rulings.Case Title: ONE RINCON HILL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. MOHSIN AHMMED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 23, 2015, line 4. CROSS DEFENDANT PRO SOLUTIONS, ONE RINCON HILL ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Though not always artfully drafted, the pro per cross-complaint is sufficient notice pleading. A general demurrer (1) does not lie to only part of a cause of action, (2) is not sustained when an improper remedy is sought and (3) is overcome by any valid cause of action regardless of what it may be labeled. Special demurrers are sustained only when a complaint is so bad that the responding party cannot determine what issues must be admitted or denied. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: SBERLO FAMILY TRUST VS. TANYA PHILLIPS (3/19/12-AMENDED TO CONTRACT ACTION)
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication Of Issues
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday October 23, 2015, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT SBERLO FAMILY TRUST, SBERLO, NADINE, TRUSTEES FOR THE SBERLO FAMILY, YOEL SBERLO, URBAN PIONEER PROPERTY MANAGEMENT, INC., MICHAEL FONTI, TAYLOR LEMBI, JEFF JUROW MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively, For Summary Adjudication DENIED, Moving party failed to comply with requirements of CCP 437c(b)(1) and CRC 3.1350(b) and (d). The Notice of Motion indicates that the motion is brought on the "grounds that, based on all inferences reasonable deductible from the evidence, one or more (or all) of the six causes of action alleged in Cross-Complaint has no merit, and not triable issues of any material fact supporting Cross-Complainant's any or all of the six causes of action exist." No list of issues to be adjudicated is provided in the notice. On page 6 of Points and Authorities moving party acknowledges that the sixth cause of action has been previously dismissed, i.e. there are only five causes of action remaining in the cross-complaint. Additionally, no issues to be adjudicated are listed in the Separate Statement. Separate Statement does not identify which purported material facts support which issue/cause of action. Separate Statement does not identify evidence in support of each material fact by reference to the exhibit, title, page, and line numbers. Points and Authorities on page 10 list 5 purported issues of duty, but then go on to discuss (1) causes of action, (2) issues which do not dispose of any cause of action completely, and (3) random points of law not tied to any cause of action in particular without any reference to the previously referenced list of duties. Finally, reply almost entirely relies on a case that has not been cited in the moving papers, depriving opposing party of an opportunity to respond. = (501/RBU)
